n0.speaker: Older daughter
n0.line: Three circles, one line. I still think it's a key, not just a doodle.

choices.text: Ask what she needs from me

n1.speaker: Older daughter
n1.line: Maybe three places in the woods and one route connecting them. We should compare with the tree mark.

actions.text: Clara circles three points on her notebook map.

n2.speaker: Older daughter
n2.line: A little. But it's the good kind of scared. Like we're close to a discovery.

choices.text: Reassure her we'll do this together

n3.speaker: Older daughter
n3.line: Don't treat me like glass. Double-check me, sure—but don't dismiss me before looking.

actions.text: She slides the notebook over, waiting for your answer.

choices.text: Agree to review clues as a team
Say safety comes first, no arguments

n4.speaker: Older daughter
n4.line: Okay. Team rules then: if one of us sees a pattern, all of us verify it.

actions.text: Clara's shoulders ease for the first time tonight.

n5.speaker: Older daughter
n5.line: Right... slow down. That's what everyone says before they ignore the problem.

n0.speaker: Younger daughter
n0.line: The trees are whispering the same shape as my drawing.

choices.text: Ask what the whisper sounds like

n1.speaker: Younger daughter
n1.line: Three moons and a road. But the road is sleepy.

n2.speaker: Younger daughter
n2.line: Near the old tree. The one that looks at us.

actions.text: She points toward the darker part of the trail.

choices.text: Invite her to guide tomorrow's route
Tell her she should stay by the cabin

n3.speaker: Younger daughter
n3.line: Like a big bee trapped in a bottle, far underground.

n4.speaker: Younger daughter
n4.line: Sleepy means hidden. It's moving, but pretending not to.

actions.text: You sketch the phrase in the margin: 'moving, pretending still.'

n5.speaker: Younger daughter
n5.line: If we all listen at once, the forest gets less scary.

n6.speaker: Younger daughter
n6.line: When people say 'don't worry,' it usually means they are worried alone.

n0.speaker: Father
n0.line: I checked the locks twice. Habit, I guess.

choices.text: Ask what he is really afraid of
Show him the latest map clues

n1.speaker: Father
n1.line: I'm afraid of choosing wrong and calling it protection.

n2.speaker: Father
n2.line: These lines... they're survey logic, not superstition. Someone planned this.

actions.text: Tomás traces the route with a grease-stained finger.

n3.speaker: Father
n3.line: Rest can wait. If the forest is changing overnight, we move at dawn.
